name: James Whitcomb Riley
abstract: James Whitcomb Riley (October 7, 1849 – July 22, 1916) was an American writer, poet, and best-selling author. During his lifetime he was known as the "Hoosier Poet" and "Childrens' Poet" for his dialect works and his poetry for children.

works_created.t: Like His Mother Used to Make

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: James Whitcomb Riley was a homespun Midwestern poet. He was hip. He knew a lot.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
